blocks.body: Past Simple vs Past Continuous — Past Simple = V2 / did + V1 for finished actions and events in the past, and Past Continuous = was / were + V-ing for an action in progress at a past moment or as background around another event.

{"level":"A2","intro":"Past Simple usually answers what happened. Past Continuous usually answers what was happening then or what was in progress at that moment.","rules":[{"label":"PAST SIMPLE","color":"blue","text":"V2 in affirmative and did + V1 in negatives and questions for finished actions and events in the past.","example":"She called me last night. / Did you see Anna yesterday?"},{"label":"PAST CONTINUOUS","color":"emerald","text":"was / were + V-ing for an action in progress at a past moment or for background around another event.","example":"She was sleeping at 11 p.m. / We were walking when it started to rain."},{"label":"KEY QUESTION","color":"violet","text":"Ask yourself: what happened? or what was happening then?","example":"finished event / action in progress at that time"}]}

{"title":"1. Quick comparison","intro":"Both tenses talk about the past, but the focus is different:","rows":[{"en":"Past Simple","ua":"a finished action or event in the past","note":"I visited my aunt yesterday."},{"en":"Past Continuous","ua":"an action in progress at a past moment","note":"I was visiting my aunt at 8 p.m."},{"en":"yesterday / last night / two days ago","ua":"often point to Past Simple","note":"We watched a film last night."},{"en":"at 8 p.m. / at that moment / this time yesterday","ua":"often point to Past Continuous","note":"We were watching a film at 8 p.m."},{"en":"when + short event","ua":"often introduces the completed action in Past Simple","note":"I was cooking when the lights went out."},{"en":"while + longer activity","ua":"often introduces the background action in Past Continuous","note":"While I was cooking, my brother was setting the table."},{"en":"stative verbs","ua":"usually stay out of Past Continuous","note":"I knew the answer."}],"warning":"If you answer what happened? or talk about a finished event, choose Past Simple. If you answer what was happening then? or show background at a past moment, choose Past Continuous."}

{"title":"2. Forms of both tenses","intro":"Compare the basic forms for affirmative, negative, and question sentences.","items":[{"label":"Past Simple","title":"Affirmative","subtitle":"Subject + V2: I worked. / She went."},{"label":"Past Simple","title":"Negative","subtitle":"Subject + did not + V1: They did not work. / He did not go."},{"label":"Past Simple","title":"Question","subtitle":"Did + subject + V1? Did you work yesterday? / Did she go out?"},{"label":"Past Continuous","title":"Affirmative","subtitle":"Subject + was / were + V-ing: I was reading. / They were waiting."},{"label":"Past Continuous","title":"Negative","subtitle":"Subject + was not / were not + V-ing: He wasn't sleeping."},{"label":"Past Continuous","title":"Question","subtitle":"Was / Were + subject + V-ing? Were they working at 8 p.m.?"}]}

{"title":"3. What decides the tense choice","sections":[{"label":"Finished action or past event","color":"blue","description":"Choose Past Simple for a completed action, event, or sequence in the past. Markers like yesterday, last night, and ago often point to this tense.","examples":[{"en":"I cleaned my room yesterday.","ua":"The action is finished in the past."},{"en":"She phoned me two minutes ago.","ua":"We ask what happened, not what was in progress."}]},{"label":"Action in progress at a past moment","color":"emerald","description":"Choose Past Continuous when the action was in progress at 8 p.m., at that moment, or this time yesterday.","examples":[{"en":"At 8 p.m., I was doing my homework.","ua":"The focus is on the action in progress then."},{"en":"At that moment, they were waiting outside.","ua":"We show what was happening at that time."}]},{"label":"Background action + main event","color":"violet","description":"Use Past Continuous for the longer background action and Past Simple for the main event that happened during it.","examples":[{"en":"I was taking a shower when the phone rang.","ua":"The shower was background; the phone call was the main event."},{"en":"We were walking home when it started to rain.","ua":"One completed action interrupted another action in progress."}]},{"label":"When and while","color":"amber","description":"when often introduces the shorter completed event, and while often introduces the longer action in progress.","examples":[{"en":"She was reading when her friend arrived.","ua":"Past Continuous + when + Past Simple is a common pattern."},{"en":"While she was reading, her friend was making tea.","ua":"while often introduces background or parallel actions."}]},{"label":"Same situation, different focus","color":"teal","description":"The context can be similar, but the meaning changes: a finished event or an action in progress at that time.","examples":[{"en":"I watched a film last night.","ua":"This answers: what happened last night?"},{"en":"At 9 p.m. last night, I was watching a film.","ua":"This answers: what was happening at 9 p.m.?"}],"note":"Past Simple often answers What happened?, and Past Continuous often answers What was happening then?"}]}

{"title":"4. Typical mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"blue","title":"Using Past Simple for an action in progress at a past moment","wrong":"❌ At 8 p.m., I watched TV.","right":"✅ At 8 p.m., I was watching TV."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"emerald","title":"Using Past Continuous for a finished action with yesterday or ago","wrong":"❌ Yesterday I was clean my room.","right":"✅ Yesterday I cleaned my room."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"violet","title":"Missing the background + interruption contrast","wrong":"❌ I cooked dinner when the lights went out.","right":"✅ I was cooking dinner when the lights went out."},{"label":"Mistake 4","color":"amber","title":"Using continuous with stative verbs","wrong":"❌ I was knowing the answer at that moment.","right":"✅ I knew the answer at that moment."}]}

{"title":"5. Stative verbs and an important note","items":["know, want, need, believe, and understand usually do not go in Past Continuous.","Say I knew the answer, not I was knowing the answer.","For A2, keep the simple rule: Past Continuous is best for actions and processes, not for every past verb.","If the verb describes a state, Past Simple is often the natural choice, even when you mention a past moment."]}

{"title":"6. A short decision guide","items":["Is it a finished action or event in the past? → choose Past Simple.","Do you see yesterday, last night, or ago with a finished event? → usually choose Past Simple.","Was the action in progress at 8 p.m., at that moment, or this time yesterday? → choose Past Continuous.","Did one short event interrupt a longer action? → use Past Continuous for the background and Past Simple for the interrupting event.","Is the verb stative, like know, want, or need? → usually stay with Past Simple."]}

{"title":"7.
